test: needle-in-haystack at depth, max_model_len=262144 (all retrieved correctly)

typical_first_request_cold.note: realistic harness baseline (system prompt + tools + history)

cold_oneshot_200k_no_cache.note: worst case — fresh 200k+ blob in one request

takeaway: Realistic first request (~39k tok) prefills in ~10s. With --enable-prefix-caching, context that grows incrementally is cached: each turn prefills only new tokens, re-reads ~instant. The multi-minute figure is a cold 200k+ one-shot paste (rare). Sliding-window attention keeps long-context KV memory cheap; cost is prefill compute.
